commit.message:
Initial commit: Conference Bingo app

Interactive bingo game for conference talks. Attendees get randomized 5x5
cards with talk-themed squares and tap them as events happen. First to
complete a row, column, or diagonal wins with confetti!

Features:
- Randomized cards from 48 RHEL/Azure/Copilot themed squares
- Live winners feed across all players
- Confetti celebration on bingo
- Admin panel for managing squares and resetting games
- 12-hour session cookies
- Mobile-first dark theme UI
- Flask + PostgreSQL + vanilla JS

commit.message:
Fix cyberpunk performance bottlenecks

Key fixes:
- Digital rain: moved animation from 300 individual spans to 12
  column divs (300 CSS animations → 12). Spans are now static text.
- Title: removed 2 of 3 simultaneous infinite animations (flicker
  + glitchBig), kept 1 slower glitch (8s vs 4s)
- Locked tiles: removed borderPulse infinite animation from every
  locked tile (was running on ~20 tiles simultaneously)
- Classified banner: removed infinite blink animation (was running
  even when banner hidden)
- Scanline overlay: added contain:strict to prevent repaint cascade
- Neon glow cursor: throttled with requestAnimationFrame (was
  updating on every mousemove event)
- Reduced scanline opacity 0.08→0.06

Before: 11 infinite animations + 300 span animations + 2 unthrottled
        mousemove handlers
After:  8 infinite animations + 0 span animations + 1 rAF-throttled
        mousemove handler

Add support for flag negation pattern (tristate) (#11)

* Add support for flag negation pattern

Flags with `negate="--no-flag"` now work correctly:
- Bool flags with `default=#true` initialize to Bool(true) instead of false
- When Bool(true) + has negate + default is true: emit nothing (already default)
- When Bool(true) + has negate + default is false: emit positive flag (--flag)
- When Bool(false) + has negate: emit the negate string (e.g. --no-color)

Added `flag "--color" negate="--no-color" default=#true` to the run command
in fixtures/sample.usage.kdl as a real-world example.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tristate NegBool variant for negatable flags

Flags with a negate field now use FlagValue::NegBool(Option<bool>) instead
of FlagValue::Bool. This supports three states:
- None: omitted (use default behavior, no flag emitted)
- Some(true): explicitly on (emits --flag)
- Some(false): explicitly off (emits negate string like --no-color)

UI shows ○ for omitted, ✓ for on, ✗ for off. Enter/Space cycles through
all three states. Backspace resets to omitted.

The negate string is displayed after the flag name (e.g. --color / --no-color)
in subdued text. Updated tests for tristate cycling, command output, and
backspace reset behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update docs for negatable flag tristate support

Document the NegBool(Option<bool>) variant, tristate cycling behavior,
UI indicators (○/✓/✗), negate string display, and keyboard interactions
for negatable flags across REQUIREMENTS.md, SPECIFICATION.md, and
IMPLEMENTATION.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Highlight negate string when negated flag is active

When NegBool(Some(false)), show the negate string (e.g. --no-color) in
flag highlight color and subdue the positive flag name (--color) to help
text color. This makes it visually clear which flag variant is currently
active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve negate flag UX: subdued separator, click-to-negate

- The " / " separator between flag and negate string is always subdued
  (help color), regardless of active state
- The negate string itself is highlighted (flag color) when active,
  subdued otherwise
- Clicking on the negate string (e.g. --no-color) directly sets the flag
  to the negated state instead of toggling through --color first
  - Clicking again on an already-negated negate string resets it to omitted
- Track negate string column positions (flag_negate_cols) during rendering
  for accurate mouse hit-testing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refine negatable flag click behavior for consistency

Each clickable region of a negatable flag row now has distinct semantics:
- Icon (○/✓/✗): cycles through all three states (same as Enter/Space)
- Positive flag name (e.g. --color): sets to on, or unsets if already on
- Negate string (e.g. --no-color): sets to off, or unsets if already off

Actions are immediate on first click (no need to focus first), making the
interaction feel direct and consistent. Non-negatable bool flags retain
the existing focus-then-activate behavior.

Also update SPECIFICATION.md mouse interaction table to document this.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: unify TemplateArgument/TemplateTypeArg and migrate template collections to InlineVector (#844)

* perf: migrate template param collections from std::vector to InlineVector<T, 4>

Replace std::vector with InlineVector<T, 4> for template parameter name
and substitution collections. This avoids heap allocation in the common
case of 1-4 template parameters.

Key changes:
- Parser::current_template_param_names_ → InlineVector<StringHandle, 4>
- Parser::template_param_substitutions_ → InlineVector<TemplateParamSubstitution, 4>
- DelayedFunctionBody::template_param_names → InlineVector<StringHandle, 4>
- DeferredTemplateMemberBody::template_param_names → InlineVector<StringHandle, 4>
- OuterTemplateBinding::param_names/param_args → InlineVector
- OutOfLineMemberFunction::template_param_names/inner → InlineVector
- OutOfLineMemberVariable/OutOfLineNestedClass → InlineVector
- TemplateAliasNode::template_param_names_ → InlineVector
- TemplateRegistry::template_parameters_ storage → InlineVector
- All related function signatures and local variables updated
- InlineVector iterator upgraded to full random access iterator
- InlineVector gains std::vector conversion constructors/assignments
- Added regression test for dependent_member_alias_multi_param

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: extend InlineVector migration to more template-related collections

- Convert remaining local param_names vectors in Lazy.cpp, MemberFunc.cpp, ClassTemplate.cpp
- Convert TemplatePattern::pattern_args to InlineVector<TemplateTypeArg, 4>
- Convert SpecializationKey::template_args to InlineVector<TemplateTypeArg, 4>
- Convert OutOfLineNestedClass::specialization_args to InlineVector<TemplateTypeArg, 4>
- Convert local pattern_args in ClassTemplate.cpp to InlineVector

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update refactoring proposal with InlineVector migration and known issue investigation

- Task 13: Document InlineVector migration details (fields, signatures, locals changed)
- KI-1: Document resolve_dependent_member_alias investigation result (not a bug)
- Fix code review feedback: add reserve() to InlineVector std::vector constructors
- Simplify redundant copy construction in TemplateRegistry_Registry.h

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: unify TemplateArgument and TemplateTypeArg, remove old struct and conversion shims

* fix: normalize Bool/Int in hash functions to match operator== contract

TemplateTypeArg::operator== treats Bool and Int as interchangeable for
value parameters (C++ non-type template parameter rule), but hash()
hashed base_type directly without this normalization - violating the
invariant that equal objects must produce equal hashes.

This caused InstantiationQueue hash maps (in_progress_, completed_,
failed_) to potentially miss lookups when Bool and Int value args were
mixed, leading to duplicate instantiations or missed cycle detection.

Fix: normalize Bool to Int in hash(), toHashString(), and
TemplateTypeArgHash::operator() when is_value is true, to match
the interchangeability already implemented in operator==.

commit.message:
Remove ADS-B heading dependency from airway matching

Origin + destination are known from adsbdb — the route is deterministic from
those alone. The aircraft's live ADS-B track was the wrong reference: it
includes wind crab, ATC deviations, and climb headings unrelated to the route.

Changes:
- GetAirwayPath: remove acHeadingDeg parameter; compute reference bearing
  internally as BearingDeg(acLat, acLon, destLat, destLon) each chain iteration
- INavigraphNavDataService: update interface to match (no acHeadingDeg param)
- PredictedPathService: remove 'if (acHeading.HasValue)' gate — always attempt
  airway snapping when position + destination are known; remove acHeading var;
  simplify fallback log (no longer shows heading degrees)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: live execution panel, CLI JSON extraction, ledger init (#16)

* feat: live execution panel, JSON extraction, ledger init

Three improvements based on real-world testing:

1. Live execution panel: Agent output is now streamed in real-time
   to a bordered panel (last 8 lines) in the terminal using ANSI
   escape codes. The panel clears when the step completes.
   - Added EnvironmentExecOptions with onStdout/onStderr callbacks
   - LocalEnvironment uses child_process.spawn for streaming mode
   - DefaultReporter and VerboseReporter use new LivePanel class

2. CLI judge JSON extraction: When a CLI model (e.g., Copilot)
   wraps JSON in natural language, extractJsonFromText() now finds
   the JSON block via ```json fences or brace matching.

3. Ledger initialization: cli.ts now calls ledger.initialize()
   before running tests, fixing ENOENT for JsonLedger when the
   .agenteval/ directory doesn't exist yet.

* fix: use globalThis singletons for cross-instance test registry (#15)

When jiti loads user eval files, `import { test } from "agent-eval"`
resolves to a separate module instance from the CLI's internal import.
This causes test registrations to be lost (0 tests found).

Fix by replacing module-level state with globalThis-backed singletons
using Symbol.for() keys, ensuring a true singleton regardless of how
many module instances exist.

commit.message:
cargo: prune deps/features (#8060)

* cargo: remove unused features, fix dev-deps, unify nusb version

- clap: remove unused 'cargo' feature (crate_version!/crate_name! macros
  not used anywhere in the workspace)
- neon: remove unused 'futures' and 'tokio' features (ballot-interpreter
  has no async neon exports and no Channel usage)
- barcode-scanner-daemon: move proptest and tokio-test to [dev-dependencies]
  since they are only used in #[cfg(test)] blocks
- pdi-scanner: use workspace nusb instead of pinning a different version;
  bump workspace nusb from 0.1.13 to 0.1.14 to match what pdi-scanner needed

These changes reduce the amount of code rust-analyzer must analyze and
eliminate unnecessary compile-time dependencies from release builds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* cargo: remove unused deps, deduplicate features, clean up manifests

Unused dependencies removed:
- pdi-scanner: crossterm, psutil, anyhow, libc, pretty_env_logger,
  chrono, crc16, rand (none referenced anywhere in source or examples)
- ballot-interpreter: bitstream-io (only used transitively via types-rs),
  pretty_env_logger (log is used but the logger is never initialized)
- fai-100-controller: hex (not referenced in source)

Non-idiomatic patterns fixed:
- accessible-controller, fai-100-controller, pat-device-input: remove
  redundant `features = ["derive"]` on clap since the workspace spec
  already includes it; use bare `{ workspace = true }` instead
- pdi-scanner: move serde_bytes into [workspace.dependencies] so all
  deps use workspace = true consistently

Cargo.toml boilerplate removed:
- Remove "See more keys" cargo-new template comments from daemon-utils,
  pat-device-input, types-rs, ballot-interpreter, pdi-scanner

These changes reduce the number of crates rust-analyzer must index,
which should improve its startup and analysis time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ballot-interpreter: remove imageproc dependency

imageproc was the only source of nalgebra (and the simba/typenum/
matrixmultiply ecosystem) in the workspace. With the contours algorithm
already removed, only a small set of imageproc functions remained in use.

Replace them with local implementations:
- contrast::otsu_level and threshold -> image_utils.rs
- drawing::draw_filled_rect_mut, draw_text_mut, text_size,
  draw_hollow_rect_mut, draw_line_segment_mut -> new draw_utils.rs
  (using ab_glyph directly for text rendering)
- imageproc::rect::Rect -> use our own Rect type throughout,
  removing the imageproc_rect_from_rect conversion helper

The one test that used imageproc for image rotation is updated to load
pre-generated rotated fixtures (blank-ballot-p3/p4-rotated-1deg.jpg)
instead of computing the rotation at test time.

Net result: 30 crates removed from Cargo.lock (nalgebra, simba, typenum,
matrixmultiply, safe_arch, wide, approx, num-complex, num-iter,
rand ecosystem, imageproc, and more).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ballot-interpreter: fix layout_glyphs kern bugs; fix code review nits

Three bugs in `layout_glyphs` (from PR review on #8060):
1. Kern arguments were reversed: `font.kern(glyph_id, last_id)` should
   be `font.kern(last_id, glyph_id)`.
2. Kern was applied after `h_advance` and glyph positioning — it must
   be applied before the glyph is placed so the position is correct.
3. `last` was only updated inside the `if let Some(g) = outline_glyph`
   block, so glyphs without outlines (e.g. spaces) never updated `last`,
   causing kerning to silently skip space-adjacent pairs.

Also address two other review nits:
- Fix misleading section comment in draw_utils.rs (was "Contrast helpers",
  now "Basic drawing helpers").
- Move `serde_bytes` in workspace Cargo.toml to its correct alphabetical
  position (between `serde` and `serde_json`).

Note: Inconsolata-Regular.ttf has zero kern values for all glyph pairs
(it is a monospace font), so the visual output of debug images is
unchanged by this fix. The correction matters for correctness should the
font ever be swapped for a proportional typeface.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix airway search: endpoint coordinate filter + wider search box + better logging

Root cause: the SQL query used fragment-level bounding boxes (can span 1000km+)
so segments far from the aircraft were returned; aircraft mid-segment had both
endpoints outside the tiny 78km search box so nothing was ever found.

Changes:
- NavigraphNavDataService: add actual from/to endpoint coordinate filter to SQL
  so only segments with at least one endpoint near the aircraft are returned
- Widen SearchHalfDeg from 0.7/1.0° to 2.5/3.5° (≈278km) to cover mid-segment
  positions (EU airways have 55-185km waypoint spacing)
- Add diagnostic console logging: initial params, chain result per iteration,
  candidate airway name/score, segs scanned — visible in journalctl
- TelegramCommandListener: include NavDataLog in /plot summary response so
  route diagnosis is visible directly in Telegram without checking journalctl

commit.message:
fix: iOS rendering with deferred RiveHostView approach

MAUI's layout system sets platform view frames to zero during initial
layout, which prevents RiveView's Metal renderer from initializing
properly. The RiveHostView container defers creating the ViewModel and
RiveView until the container has a valid frame AND is in a window,
mirroring how the native SDK expects views to be set up.

Also removes the CG renderer workaround (no longer needed) and cleans
up the test native button from the sample app.

commit.message:
perf: reduce allocations in token stream formatting

# PR Glass Cockpit

Make review boring. Make truth cheap.

## 💡 Summary
Added explicit capacity hints to String allocations in `tokmd-format`
rendering functions. This avoids repeated memory reallocations when
rendering reports for repositories with many files or languages.

## 🎯 Why (perf bottleneck)
Functions like `render_lang_md`, `render_module_md`, and
`render_diff_md` were initializing with `String::new()` and then
repeatedly extending the string in loops based on row data. This causes
intermediate memory reallocations which hurts runtime performance and
CPU utilization on larger inputs.

## 📊 Proof (before/after)
Performance was measured using `cargo bench -p tokmd-format` with tables
of 1000 rows (`render_lang_tsv_1000`, `render_module_tsv_1000`,
`render_module_md_1000`):

- `render_module_tsv_1000` improved from ~174 µs to ~161 µs (-14.5% to
-1.2%)
- `render_lang_tsv_1000` improved from ~185 µs to ~171 µs (-9.6% to
-7.3%)
- `render_module_md_1000` improved from ~190 µs to ~172 µs (-10.4% to
-9.5%)

## 🧭 Options considered
### Option A (recommended)
- What it is: Use `String::with_capacity` based on a heuristic of rows
and column character count.
- Why it fits this repo: Maintains absolute output determinism, is
highly isolated (SRP), and doesn't change API signatures.
- Trade-offs: Requires a heuristic size which might over-allocate
slightly, but prevents expensive `O(N)` reallocations.

### Option B
- What it is: Refactor formatting to pass a `&mut dyn Write` and stream
directly to output.
- When to choose it instead: If peak memory usage is the critical issue
and allocating the full formatted string is unacceptable.
- Trade-offs: Would require changing API signatures across the
`tokmd-format` crate.

## ✅ Decision
Option A was chosen as it delivers an easy-to-trust performance win
without altering public APIs or structure.

## 🧱 Changes made (SRP)
- `crates/tokmd-format/src/lib.rs`
- Replaced `String::new()` with `String::with_capacity()` using length
heuristics in Markdown and TSV renderers.

## 🧪 Verification receipts
- `cargo bench -p tokmd-format` (verified improvements)
- `cargo build`
- `CI=true cargo test --verbose` (Pass)
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` (Pass)
- `cargo fmt -- --check` (Pass)

## 🧭 Telemetry
- Change shape: Shallow string pre-allocation
- Blast radius (API / IO / format stability / concurrency): Purely
internal implementation detail. No API or format changes.
- Risk class + why: Extremely low. Determinism verified by snapshot
tests.
- Rollback: Revert to `String::new()`
- Merge-confidence gates: standard gates.

## 🗂️ .jules updates
- Initialized `.jules/policy/scheduled_tasks.json` and basic `.jules`
runbooks for Bolt.
- Created `20260303T132005Z.json` envelope, `2026-03-03.md` run log, and
updated `ledger.json` in `.jules/bolt/`.

---

[9006619675249841418](https://jules.google.com/task/9006619675249841418)
started by @EffortlessSteven*

commit.message:
test(core,ffi): deep tests for core FFI and workflow layer

Wave 43: 93 tests for tokmd-core FFI entrypoint (58 tests) and
tokmd-ffi-envelope (35 tests).

**tokmd-core/tests/ffi_w43.rs** (58 tests):
- Version mode: ok response, schema_version, idempotency
- Lang mode: receipt structure, mode field, rows, tool info, scan/args
metadata, top/files params
- Module mode: receipt structure, schema_version, rows
- Export mode: receipt structure, rows with path/lang fields
- Invalid mode: error envelope, case sensitivity, spaces, unicode
- Invalid JSON: garbage, empty, truncated, bare array
- Envelope contract: ok is always boolean, success has data not error,
error has code+message
- Strict settings validation: invalid types for
top/files/children/format/redact/hidden/paths/config
- Nested scan object parsing
- Diff mode: missing from/to, self-diff zero deltas
- Feature-gated: analyze/cockpit not_implemented without features
- Error code snake_case invariant, non-empty messages

**tokmd-ffi-envelope/tests/deep_w43.rs** (35 tests):
- Ok envelope extraction: object, nested, null, missing data
- Error envelope: upstream error, missing error obj, null error, data
ignored
- Serde roundtrip: object, array, string, numeric precision, unicode
- Live integration: schema_version in version/lang envelopes, error
propagation
- parse_envelope edge cases: bare number/bool, empty, partial
- format_error_message: numeric code, empty strings, boolean values
- Error traits: Display, Clone, Debug, PartialEq/Ne
- Type coercion: ok as string/integer/missing treated as error
- Consistency: two-step vs one-step extraction
